description_long: This is a compact, rectangular computer keyboard with a standard array of black and blue keycaps, each labeled with white alphanumeric and function symbols. The keyboard appears to be missing its outer casing, exposing the underlying green printed circuit board (PCB) and some internal wiring, especially along the left side. The keycaps are arranged in a typical staggered layout, with the blue keys likely representing function or modifier keys. The surface of the keyboard shows signs of wear, with some debris and discoloration visible on the PCB and between the keys. The object is relatively low in profile, with a flat base and slightly raised key rows. No visible branding or manufacturer logo is present, and there is no attached cable or wireless receiver.
description: A compact, exposed computer keyboard with black and blue keycaps on a visible green circuit board, showing signs of wear and missing its outer casing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full key layout, exposed PCB, and visible debris between the keys.
description_view_1: Left-side view highlighting the blue key row and exposed wiring along the edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tops of the keycaps and the flat, open back of the keyboard.
description_view_3: Right-side view showing the staggered key arrangement and more exposed PCB components.
